ops.title: daemonkit decision: hard cut, no backwards compat
ops.body:
**Decision (user, 2026-07-17):** fusekit hard-deletes `proc/`, `service/`, `appgroup/`, `version/` when they move to daemonkit — no re-exports, no alias shims, no migration code anywhere in the fleet. Downstream repos (cc-pool: ~20 files import the moved packages; cc-notes) stay pinned to the last pre-cut fusekit until their own flip commit swaps imports to daemonkit + bumps fusekit atomically.

**Gate:** the deleting fusekit release is tagged only after every consumer's flip commit exists and is green under a local `go.work`. Consumers never auto-update fusekit; a pre-flip `go get -u` breaks loudly — acceptable in a closed fleet, the loud break IS the detection.

**Sentinel identity:** consumers re-aliasing sentinels (`var ErrMountNotLive = fusekit.ErrMountNotLive`) must keep pointing at the same object post-move — moved sentinels live in exactly one package; importers update.
